basics.name: Neha Bhomia
basics.label: Data Lead & Applications Systems Programmer Analyst
basics.summary: Data and systems professional at the intersection of healthcare, analytics, and technical implementation. Focused on building reliable data pipelines, validating complex healthcare datasets, automating reporting workflows, and translating technical findings into actionable information for clinical, operational, and administrative stakeholders.

work.name: University of Michigan School of Information
work.summary: Teaching graduate-level courses at the School of Information, handling logistics from content creation to lecturing, and providing student support with regular assessments.
highlights: Courses Taught: Health Data Analysis, Introduction to Applied Data Science, Milestone course, Capstone project course, Machine Learning in Health, Machine Learning Pipelines, Health Informatics Seminar

work.name: School of Information Computing Services, University of Michigan
work.summary: Collaborating with academic and administrative units to provide actionable data insights for decision-making, strategic planning, and curriculum development.
highlights: Analyze academic performance data, enrollment trends, and student demographics
Develop automation scripts to streamline data processing and reporting tasks
Establish and maintain data pipelines for integrating external vendor software (EAB, CourseDog, Lattice)
Provide full stack development support including the Promotions and Tenure website for the Regents office
Ensure data integrity through regular audits and validation checks

work.name: Michigan Collaborative for Type 2 Diabetes (MCT2D)
work.summary: Leading technical research projects by identifying clinical requirements, collaborating with stakeholders, and building data pipelines for collaborative quality initiatives at Michigan Medicine.
highlights: Create data pipelines and flows for collaborative quality initiatives (CQIs)
Write, test, and implement programs to clean, merge, analyze, and maintain large complex datasets
Work with health system stakeholders to automate processes for managing data and producing reports
Create user-friendly data visualizations for end users and clinicians
Access SQL databases for required views, joins, and filters for analysis

work.name: Michigan Radiation Oncology Quality Consortium (MROQC)
work.summary: Building, testing, debugging, and running clinical workflows to enhance data analysis for the statewide collaborative.
highlights: Analyze data collected by specialists across Michigan for quality improvement on breast cancer project
Built custom web application using HTML5, CSS3 & JavaScript for clinicians to determine patient follow-up dates

work.name: Department of Family Medicine, Michigan Medicine
work.summary: Worked with data flows and API methods for survey extraction in support of research at Family Medicine.
highlights: Wrote algorithms in Python to detect correlation between step counts and heart rates for 10M+ entries
Built clinical research platforms using Salesforce, AWS, Twilio, Flask, Qualtrics
Built data pipelines to pull EHR data into Salesforce for screening potential research participants
Utilized HL7 as a data transfer format between diverse systems

volunteer.organization: Washtenaw County Health Department & School of Information, University of Michigan
MI Lighthouse, State of Michigan & University of Michigan
Ann Arbor Data Dive, University of Michigan

volunteer.position: City Level Administrator & ESL Volunteer Teacher

education.institution: University of Michigan, School of Information

highlights: Former Registered and Licensed Practitioner under Karnataka State Dental Council (KSDC)

certificates.name: Introduction to Computer Science and Programming using Python

projects.name: ML Models for Radiotherapy Factors and Toxicity Correlation
projects.summary: Built Machine Learning models to analyze correlations between radiotherapy metrics (Beam Modality, Beam Energy) and adverse effects (dermatitis, pain). Applied Logistic Regression, Image Processing, Gaussian Naive Bayes, and Random Forest Decision Trees.
highlights: Logistic Regression
Random Forest
Image Processing

projects.name: Mortality Rate Prediction for Myocardial Infarction
projects.summary: Worked with the WHO MONICA dataset to predict mortality rate for patients with Myocardial Infarction. Conducted exploratory data analysis of 6,000+ patient records using Pandas and NumPy.
highlights: XGBoost
SVM
Neural Networks
KNN
